---
title: "10B CODE BASE"
author: "IZZA KHAN"
format: html
---

```{R}
library(jsonlite)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
```

```{R}
url <- "https://api.nobelprize.org/v1/laureate.json"
data_raw <- fromJSON(url)
```

```{R}
names(data_raw)
```

```{R}
laureates <- data_raw$laureates
```

```{R}
laureates_df <- as.data.frame(laureates)
head(laureates_df)
```

```{R}
laureates_df <- laureates_df %>%
  mutate(
    bornCountry = as.character(bornCountry),
    diedCountry = as.character(diedCountry)
  )
```

QUESTION 1
```{R}
q1 <- laureates_df %>%
  group_by(bornCountry) %>%
  summarise(count = n()) %>%
  arrange(desc(count)) %>%
  head(10)

q1
```
Answer:
The results show that a small number of countries dominate Nobel Prize winners, with the highest counts appearing at the top of the table.

QUESTION 2
```{R}
prizes_url <- "https://api.nobelprize.org/v1/prize.json"
prizes_raw <- fromJSON(prizes_url)
```

```{R}
prizes <- prizes_raw$prizes
prizes_df <- as.data.frame(prizes)
head(prizes_df)
```

```{R}
q2 <- prizes_df %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(count = n())
```

```{R}
ggplot(q2, aes(x = as.numeric(year), y = count)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(title = "Nobel Prizes Over Time", x = "Year", y = "Count")
```
Answer:
The number of Nobel Prizes awarded changes over time, showing fluctuations across different years.

QUESTION 3
```{R}
q3 <- prizes_df %>%
  group_by(category) %>%
  summarise(count = n()) %>%
  arrange(desc(count))

q3
```
Answer:
Some categories appear more frequently than others, indicating differences in how often prizes are awarded by field.

QUESTION 4
```{R}
q4 <- laureates_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(bornCountry) & !is.na(diedCountry)) %>%
  filter(bornCountry != diedCountry) %>%
  group_by(bornCountry, diedCountry) %>%
  summarise(count = n()) %>%
  arrange(desc(count)) %>%
  head(10)

q4
```
Answer:
This analysis highlights cases where Nobel laureates were born in one country but later associated with another, reflecting migration and global movement of talent.
